title: Tabernaemontana johnstonii
intro: Tabernaemontana johnstonii, also known as Johnston's wood rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the tropical regions of Madagascar. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antihypertensive properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and high blood pressure. The bioactive constituents of Tabernaemontana johnstonii include alkaloids, such as johnstonine and tabernanthine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used to make a tincture, which is used to treat various health conditions.
